research.description: Research the topic '{topic}'. Your deliverable is a comprehensive research notes file at workspace/research_notes.md. Structure your notes under these sections: (1) Overview and Key Definitions, (2) Major Frameworks and Their Architecture, (3) Comparative Analysis and Trade-offs, (4) Production Patterns and Challenges, (5) Bibliography. Cite at least 8 sources using [AuthorYear] inline citation format. Minimum 1500 words total.
research.expected_output: A structured Markdown file at workspace/research_notes.md with 5 sections, minimum 1500 words, and at least 8 [AuthorYear] inline citations with full references in the Bibliography section.

write.description: Using the research notes at workspace/research_notes.md, write 6 Markdown chapters for an article on '{topic}'. Save each chapter to workspace/chapters/. Chapters: ch01_introduction.md (~400 words, introduce the topic), ch02_architectures.md (~500 words, agent architectures and topologies), ch03_frameworks.md (~500 words, LangChain vs CrewAI vs LangGraph comparison with a Markdown table), ch04_production.md (~400 words, production patterns including a mathematical relationship you mark with [NEEDS_FORMULA]), ch05_bidi.md (~400 words, write at least 3 full paragraphs in Hebrew mixed with English technical terms), ch06_casestudy.md (~300 words, case study of this pipeline). Each chapter must include at least 2 inline citations.
write.expected_output: 6 Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_introduction.md, ch02_architectures.md, ch03_frameworks.md (with comparison table), ch04_production.md (with [NEEDS_FORMULA] marker), ch05_bidi.md (with Hebrew paragraphs), ch06_casestudy.md.

edit.description: Review and polish all 6 chapters in workspace/chapters/. For each chapter: (1) enforce consistent terminology, (2) verify all [AuthorYear] citations are defined in the research notes bibliography, (3) ensure tables use pipe | syntax for clean LaTeX conversion, (4) verify ch05_bidi has at least 3 Hebrew paragraphs, (5) mark any mathematical relationship not yet marked as [NEEDS_FORMULA]. Save each polished chapter as ch0N_edited.md in workspace/chapters/.
edit.expected_output: 6 edited Markdown files in workspace/chapters/: ch01_edited.md through ch06_edited.md. Each improved for clarity, citation correctness, and LaTeX readiness.

latex_ch01.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
2. Tables: use ONLY p{Xcm} column types. NEVER bare 'l', 'r', or 'c'.
3. Citations: use keys from this set EXACTLY: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
4. No markdown fences (```latex), no explanatory text after the final LaTeX line.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 1 for an article on Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns in AI Systems.
Start with: \chapter{Introduction to Multi-Agent Orchestration}\label{ch:intro}
Include 3-4 \section{} headings. Minimum 400 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Describe: what multi-agent orchestration is, why single-agent systems are insufficient for complex tasks, the key roles (researcher, writer, editor, LaTeX producer), and what the article will cover.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch01.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 1 starting with \chapter{Introduction to Multi-Agent Orchestration}, with sections, citations, and no markdown.

latex_ch02.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
2. Tables: use ONLY p{Xcm} column types (e.g. p{3.2cm}). NEVER bare 'l', 'r', or 'c'.
3. Citations: use keys from this set EXACTLY: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
4. No markdown fences, no explanatory text after the final LaTeX line.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 2 for an article on Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns in AI Systems.
Start with: \chapter{Agent Architectures and Topologies}\label{ch:architectures}
Include sections on: Sequential Pipelines, Hierarchical Manager-Worker, Peer-to-Peer Meshes, Hybrid Orchestrators, Topology Comparison.
After the first paragraph, include: \begin{figure}[H]\centering\resizebox{\textwidth}{!}{\input{figures/crew_architecture.tikz}}\caption{CrewAI multi-agent architecture.}\label{fig:crew_architecture}\end{figure}
Include one \begin{equation} block for pipeline latency formula: $L_{\mathrm{seq}} = \sum_{i=1}^{N}\ell_i$
Include one \begin{longtable} with p{} column widths comparing topologies. Minimum 4 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch02.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 2 starting with \chapter{Agent Architectures and Topologies}, including \resizebox TikZ figure and citations.

latex_ch03.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
2. Tables: use ONLY p{Xcm} column types. Total must be ≤14cm. Use @{}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}@{} for 4-col tables. NEVER bare 'l', 'r', or 'c'.
3. Citations: use keys from this set EXACTLY: LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, CrewAI2024, AutoGen2023, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Yao2023, Wang2023.
4. No markdown fences, no explanatory text after the final LaTeX line.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 3 for an article on Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns in AI Systems.
Start with: \chapter{Orchestration Frameworks}\label{ch:frameworks}
Include sections on: LangChain and LCEL, CrewAI Role-Based Agents, LangGraph Stateful Graphs, Microsoft AutoGen, Framework Comparison.
The comparison section MUST include a \begin{longtable}{@{}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}p{3.5cm}@{}} table comparing LangChain, CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen across: Architecture, Strengths, Weaknesses, Best For.
Minimum 400 words. Minimum 4 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch03.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 3 starting with \chapter{Orchestration Frameworks}, including a longtable framework comparison with p{} columns and citations.

latex_ch04.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
2. Tables: use ONLY p{Xcm} column types. NEVER bare 'l', 'r', or 'c'.
3. Citations: use keys from this set EXACTLY: Turner1986, Segal2026, CrewAI2024, LangGraph2024, Anthropic2024b, Wang2023, Yao2023, Russell2020.
4. No markdown fences, no explanatory text after the final LaTeX line.
5. CRITICAL: EVERY mathematical expression MUST use \begin{equation}...\end{equation}. NEVER write math as plain text.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 4 for an article on Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns in AI Systems.
Start with: \chapter{Production Patterns and Challenges}\label{ch:production}
Include sections on: Rate Limiting and Token Budgets, Gatekeeper Pattern, Retry and Circuit Breaker, Observability and Logging, Cost Optimization.
Include AT LEAST 3 fancy equations (\begin{equation}) for: token budget formula, cost model, retry probability.
Example equation: \begin{equation}C = \sum_{k=1}^{K}\bigl(c_{\mathrm{in}}\,t_{k}^{\mathrm{in}} + c_{\mathrm{out}}\,t_{k}^{\mathrm{out}}\bigr)\label{eq:cost}\end{equation}
Minimum 400 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch04.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 4 with at least 3 \begin{equation} blocks for production patterns math.

latex_ch05.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be valid LaTeX. First character must be '%' or '\'. NO prose before or after.
2. Tables: use ONLY p{Xcm} column types. NEVER bare 'l', 'r', or 'c'.
3. Citations: use keys from this set EXACTLY: Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, Segal2026, CrewAI2024, Anthropic2024b.
4. No markdown fences, no explanatory text after the final LaTeX line.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 5 — the Hebrew BiDi chapter. EXACT structure required:

\addtocontents{toc}{%
  \protect\etocsetstyle{chapter}{}{}{\protect\addvspace{1.0em plus 1pt}%
   \begingroup\parindent0pt\noindent\bfseries%
   \etocpage\hfill{\color{uohblue}\etocname\hspace{1em}\etocnumber}%
  \par\endgroup}{}%
  \protect\etocsetstyle{section}{}{}{\begingroup\parindent0pt\noindent\normalfont\normalsize%
     \etocpage\hspace{0.5em}\protect\hebrewdotleaders\hspace{0.5em}%
     \etocname\hspace{0.5em}\etocnumber%
  \par\endgroup}{}%
}
\hebrewheadingformat
\chapter{\texthebrew{דו-כיוניות: עברית ואנגלית במערכות סוכנים}}
\label{ch:bidi}

Hebrew sections use: \section[\texthebrew{כותרת}]{\texthebrew{כותרת}\mbox{\setLTR\enspace\thesection}}
Hebrew body: \begin{hebrew}...\end{hebrew}
English terms in Hebrew blocks: \foreignlanguage{english}{term}
After Hebrew sections (before 5.4): \addtocontents{toc}{\protect\etocstandardlines}\defaultheadingformat
Section 5.4 uses normal \section{Mixed-Language Example}.

Content: 3 Hebrew sections on BiDi typography in AI systems (meaning of bidirectionality, LuaLaTeX/polyglossia solution, challenges in production), plus one English section on mixed language example. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch05.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 5 with Hebrew RTL headings, \begin{hebrew} body paragraphs, \foreignlanguage{english}{} for Latin terms, and the exact TOC override block.

Write a complete LaTeX chapter 6 for an article on Multi-Agent Orchestration Patterns in AI Systems.
Start with: \chapter{Case Study: This Article's Production Pipeline}\label{ch:casestudy}
Include sections on: Overview, The Crew (4 agents: Researcher, Writer, Editor, LaTeXAgent), Workflow Architecture, Key Engineering Decisions, Pipeline Metrics.
Include a \begin{longtable} metrics table with columns: Metric / Value / Notes. Include rows for: pipeline agents (4), tasks defined (11), chapters generated (6), LaTeX passes (4), target PDF length (20 pages), elapsed time (<10 min with fast pipeline), Python files (≤16), test coverage (≥85%).
Minimum 300 words. Minimum 3 \cite{} citations.
Context from previous task will be appended below if available.
latex_ch06.expected_output: Valid LaTeX source for chapter 6 starting with \chapter{Case Study: This Article's Production Pipeline}.

latex_bib.description:
OUTPUT RULES (follow exactly — violations cause compilation failure):
1. Your ENTIRE response must be a valid .bib file. First character must be '@'. NO prose before or after.
2. Generate @article, @book, or @misc entries for EVERY key from this set: Russell2020, Wooldridge2009, WooldridgeJennings1995, WeiEtAl2022, Yao2023, Shinn2023, Park2023, Wang2023, Turner1986, CrewAI2024, LangChain2023, LangChain2024a, LangGraph2024, AutoGen2023, Anthropic2024b, Microsoft2025, Segal2026, Unicode2023, Polyglossia2023, AMS2023.
3. No duplicate keys. No markdown fences. No trailing text after the last '}'.

Generate a complete BibTeX file with entries for ALL 20 citation keys listed above. Each entry needs: author, title, year, and at least one of: journal/booktitle/howpublished/url.
latex_bib.expected_output: Valid BibTeX content with @article/@book/@misc entries for all 20 citation keys.
